info.name: Restful Booker
info.description:
## Restful Booker – API Test Collection

This collection contains a comprehensive suite of API tests for the [Restful Booker](https://restful-booker.herokuapp.com) platform — a sample hotel booking API commonly used for API testing practice.

The collection is organized into **5 functional folders**, covering the full booking lifecycle:

| Folder | Method(s) | Requests |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Login** | POST | 6 |
| **Create** | POST | 16 |
| **Read** | GET | 7 |
| **Update** | PUT | 12 |
| **Delete** | DELETE | 9 |

### What's covered

- **Login** – Authentication via `/auth`, including valid credentials, incorrect username/password, empty body, SQL injection prevention, and token format validation.
    
- **Create** – Booking creation via `/booking`, covering happy-path scenarios (valid data, zero price, missing optional fields) and negative cases (invalid dates, wrong data types, missing mandatory fields, unsupported headers, malformed JSON, SQL injection).
    
- **Read** – Retrieval of single and all bookings, including valid IDs, non-existent IDs, extremely large IDs, special characters, and unsupported Accept headers.
    
- **Update** – Full booking updates via PUT, testing successful updates, boundary values (minimum price), missing/invalid fields, authentication failures (no token, invalid token), and unsupported headers.
    
- **Delete** – Booking deletion with Cookie auth, Basic auth, combined auth headers, as well as rejection scenarios (no auth, invalid token, non-existent ID, already-deleted booking, invalid ID format).
    

### Key characteristics

- Tests both **positive (2xx)** and **negative (4xx)** scenarios throughout.
    
- Validates **authentication and authorization** (token-based and Basic Auth).
    
- Includes **boundary and edge-case** testing (empty strings, null values, out-of-range dates, SQL injection, special characters).
    
- Uses the `{{last_created_id}}` variable to chain requests across the Create → Read → Update → Delete flow.
